system.name: SignalLayer
system.summary: SignalLayer is a browser-based conversation augmentation application with a first-party chat surface, inline claim annotations, a conversation summary bar, a right-side analysis pane, and a modular reasoning backend.

subsystems.name: browser_app_layer
subsystems.label: Browser App Layer
responsibilities: Render the chat experience and message timeline
Decorate messages with inline badges without mutating message text
Manage hover, click, selection, and analysis pane state
Coordinate fast local analysis with async backend enrichment
Persist lightweight client cache keyed by message hash
failure_modes: Large conversations create excessive re-renders or layout shifts
Rapid message arrival causes duplicate analysis requests
Badge injection obscures message affordances on narrow screens
Client cache becomes stale when message text changes

subsystems.name: ui_component_layer
subsystems.label: UI Component Layer
responsibilities: Provide reusable React components for badges, decorators, tooltips, pane cards, and confidence bars
Enforce a dark theme with low-intrusion visual hierarchy
Surface uncertainty, evidence, and counterpoints with progressive disclosure
failure_modes: Component state diverges from app store during async updates

subsystems.name: backend_layer
subsystems.label: Backend API Layer
responsibilities: Accept analysis requests from the browser app
Coordinate claim extraction, classification, retrieval, counterpoint generation, and confidence scoring
Normalize outputs into a stable JSON contract
Serve deterministic fixtures when external model access is unavailable
failure_modes: Malformed payloads or missing context fields
Evidence corpus does not contain relevant support
Concurrent requests create duplicate work without cache coalescing

subsystems.name: ai_agent_layer
subsystems.label: AI Agent Layer
responsibilities: Run modular reasoning steps instead of a single monolithic prompt
Generate component prompts for implementation and prompt refinements
Bridge optional LLM providers with deterministic local heuristics fallback
Score confidence based on retrieval quality, claim clarity, and contradiction signals
failure_modes: Provider response is unparsable or inconsistent with schema
Heuristic fallback over-classifies soft opinions as factual claims

subsystems.name: data_layer
subsystems.label: Data Layer
responsibilities: Store the seed evidence corpus for RAG-lite retrieval
Provide hash-based memoization for analysis results
Retain chat fixtures for tests and UI previews
failure_modes: Sparse corpus yields weak evidence quality
Cache key collisions across near-identical messages
Fixture drift causes frontend and backend contract mismatches

safety: degrade gracefully on network or model failure
testing: simulate chat DOM and annotation placement
